title: Edge AI Inference
subtitle: Running ML models at the edge for real-time decisions

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Centralised AI inference is like calling head office for every decision. Edge AI inference puts a trained expert at every branch — they handle routine questions instantly, only escalating the hard ones to HQ.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Akamai's edge AI inference story combines multiple products:

- **Akamai Inference Cloud**: Hosted model serving with API access, GPU-backed
- **Neural Magic on Linode CPUs**: Sparsified models running on commodity CPU instances across 30+ regions
- **Akamai Functions**: Lightweight pre/post-processing of inference requests at the edge (prompt sanitization, response shaping)
- **EdgeWorkers**: Ultra-light classification (e.g., bot-or-not, geo-routing to nearest inference endpoint)

The pattern: EdgeWorker classifies at the PoP → Function pre-processes → Inference Cloud runs the model → Function post-processes → CDN caches where possible.

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Use cases already in production: real-time content moderation for UGC platforms, fraud scoring at checkout, personalized recommendation ranking, image classification for automated tagging. The latency requirement determines the tier: <5ms = EdgeWorker, <50ms = Function + cached model, <500ms = Inference Cloud GPU.
"Edge AI = tiered inference. Light decisions at the PoP (EdgeWorkers), medium at distributed regions (Functions), heavy on GPU (Inference Cloud). Match latency budget to tier."

quiz.question: A customer needs real-time fraud scoring with <50ms latency globally. What's the recommended architecture?

options.explain: Single region can't serve <50ms globally — too much network latency.

options.text: Sparsified model on Neural Magic (CPU Linodes) across multiple regions, with Akamai Functions for pre/post-processing
options.explain: CPU inference via Neural Magic distributes across 30+ regions. Functions handle request shaping. <50ms achievable globally.
EdgeWorkers can't run full ML models — CPU time is too bounded.
Browser inference is too slow and exposes the model.

quiz.question: What's the tiered inference pattern on Akamai's edge?

options.explain: Tiered means different compute for different latency budgets.
Match the model complexity to the compute tier. Light classification at PoPs, full inference on GPUs.
Server-side inference is the point of Akamai's offering.

options.text: Only CPU or only GPU, never mixed
options.explain: The tiered model explicitly mixes CPU and GPU tiers.

free.title: Neural Magic + Akamai edge inference patterns
